name: scales-nest
description: Read and write your end-to-end-encrypted Nest through your own AI. Your encryption key is derived locally and never leaves your machine; Nest's servers only ever see ciphertext.
long_description: Nest is your encrypted second brain (people, companies, tasks, events). This connector runs a small MCP server on your own machine. It fetches your password-wrapped key from Nest, unwraps it locally, then decrypts on read and encrypts on write right here. The Nest server only ever stores ciphertext and never receives your password, your key, or your plaintext. Mint a scoped API key in Nest (Settings then Connect your AI), paste it plus your encryption password below, and your AI can read and (with a full-control key) write your Nest data.

tools.name: list_people
tools.description: List people in your Nest, decrypted locally.

tools.name: get_person
tools.description: Get one person by id, decrypted locally.

tools.name: search_people
tools.description: Search people by decrypted name or notes.

tools.name: create_person
tools.description: Create a person; encrypted locally before it is stored.

tools.name: update_person
tools.description: Update a person; re-encrypted locally before it is stored.

tools.name: get_digest
tools.description: Get your follow-up digest (overdue and upcoming).

nest_api_key.title: Nest API key
nest_api_key.description: Your scoped key from Nest (Settings then Connect your AI). Read-only is enough to read; choose Full control to let your AI create and update records. Looks like nest_xxxx_...

nest_password.title: Nest encryption password
nest_password.description: The password you set when you turned on encryption in Nest. Used locally to unlock your key. It is stored in your operating system keychain and is never sent to Nest. Leave blank only if your account is not encrypted.

nest_api_url.title: Nest URL (advanced)
nest_api_url.description: Leave as the default unless you are testing against another Nest instance.
